workflow.name: Promote calendar events with OpenAI graphics to LinkedIn, X, and Facebook
workflow.description:
Never let an event go unpromoted. This workflow monitors your Google Calendar, generates branded promotional graphics, and schedules a staggered social media countdown across LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and Facebook.

---

## 🎯 What This Workflow Does

This template automates the full lifecycle of event promotion—from creation to multi-platform publishing.

---

### 📅 Step 1 — Calendar Trigger & Intelligence

- **Instant Trigger:** Fires when a new event is created in Google Calendar  
- **Data Enrichment:** Extracts title, location, and formatted date/time  
- **Safety Gate:** Skips events starting in less than 2 hours  

---

### 🖼️ Step 2 — Programmatic Design & CDN Hosting

- **Dynamic Graphic Creation:** Uses Edit Image node to overlay event details on a banner  
- **CDN Hosting:** Uploads the image via UploadToURL to generate a public URL  
- **Schedule Calculation:** Sets post timings at 48h, 24h, and 1h before the event  

---

### 🔁 Step 3 — Staggered Countdown & AI Copy

- **Loop Execution:** Iterates through each scheduled post time  
- **Smart Wait:** Delays execution until the exact posting time  
- **AI Captions:** Generates time-based captions using OpenAI  

---

### 📊 Step 4 — Audit Logging & Admin Alerts

- **Google Sheets Log:** Stores platform status and image URLs for each post  
- **Telegram Alerts:** Sends a final campaign summary to the admin  

---

## ✨ Key Features

- **Automated Graphic Design:** Creates visuals for every event  
- **Persistent Wait Logic:** Handles long delays within a single workflow  
- **Multi-Platform Posting:** Publishes to LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and Facebook  
- **CDN Media Bridge:** UploadToURL ensures valid public image URLs  

---

## 🔧 Setup Requirements

### Required Integrations

- **Google Calendar:** OAuth2 credentials  
- **Social Platforms:** LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Facebook Page credentials  
- **OpenAI:** API key for captions  
- **Google Sheets:** Sheet for logging  
- **UploadToURL:** API key  
- **Telegram:** Bot token and chat ID  

---

### Required Assets

- Public URL of your base banner template image  

---

Automate your event marketing. Import this template and connect your Google Calendar to start promoting events effortlessly!

parameters.content:
## 📅 Event Promotion — Auto-Generate Graphic & Schedule Posts Across Social Channels

**What this workflow does:**
When a new event is added to Google Calendar (or Eventbrite via polling), this workflow:
1. Extracts event details (name, date, time, location, description)
2. Uses the **Edit Image node** to programmatically compose a branded promotional graphic (overlaying event title + date onto a template image)
3. Uploads the graphic via **UploadToURL** to get a public CDN URL
4. Uses a **DateTime node** to calculate the optimal post schedule (48hrs, 24hrs, and 1hr before event)
5. Loops through 3 scheduled time slots using **Loop Over Items**
6. At each loop iteration, a **Wait node** pauses until the scheduled time, then fires posts to LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and Facebook simultaneously
7. Logs every post action to **Google Sheets** (platform, post time, event name, status)
8. Sends a **Telegram** confirmation message to the admin after all posts are dispatched

**Architecture (fully unique from Templates 1 & 2):**
- 📅 Google Calendar Trigger (NOT RSS / Schedule / Webhook)
- 🖼️ Edit Image Node — programmatic graphic composition
- ☁️ UploadToURL — mandatory CDN hosting of generated graphic
- 🕐 DateTime Node — calculates 3 staggered post schedules
- 🔁 Loop Over Items — iterates through each scheduled slot
- ⏳ Wait Node — pauses execution until exact post time
- 🔀 Merge Node — recombines event data + schedule data
- 📊 Google Sheets — post audit log
- 📨 Telegram — admin notification after completion

**Setup Requirements:**
1. Google Calendar OAuth2 credentials (watch a specific calendar)
2. A base event banner image stored at a public URL (used as graphic template)
3. UploadToURL endpoint configured
4. LinkedIn OAuth2 credentials
5. Twitter/X OAuth1 credentials
6. Facebook Graph API token (pages_manage_posts scope)
7. Google Sheets OAuth2 — create a sheet named `EventPostLog` with columns: EventID, EventName, Platform, ScheduledTime, PostedAt, Status, ImageURL
8. Telegram Bot Token + your Chat ID

### 📅 Step 1 — Google Calendar Trigger & Event Data Extraction
**Google Calendar Trigger:** Fires instantly when any new event is created in your watched calendar. No polling — uses Google's push notification system.
**Code Node (Parse Event):** Extracts `eventId`, `summary` (title), `description`, `location`, `startDateTime`, `endDateTime`, and the event organizer. Also derives a clean `eventDate` string and `daysUntilEvent` count used for scheduling logic downstream.
**IF Node (Future Events Only):** Skips events that start in less than 2 hours — not enough lead time to schedule meaningful promotional posts.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Edit Image, Upload & Schedule Calc

parameters.content:
### 🖼️ Step 2 — Graphic Generation & UploadToURL
**HTTP — Fetch Banner Template:** Downloads your base event banner image (stored at a fixed public URL) as binary.
**Edit Image Node:** Overlays the event title, date, and location as text onto the banner image. Uses built-in composite operations — no external design API needed.
**UploadToURL:** Uploads the composed graphic binary to your CDN. Returns a permanent public URL used in all social posts across all scheduled slots.
**DateTime Node:** Calculates exactly 3 post timestamps — 48 hours before, 24 hours before, and 1 hour before the event start time.

### 🔁 Step 3 — Loop, Wait & Scheduled Multi-Platform Posting
**Merge Node:** Joins the event data (from step 1) with the scheduled timestamps + hosted image URL (from step 2) into a single enriched item before looping.
**Loop Over Items:** Iterates over the 3 schedule slots one by one (48hr, 24hr, 1hr).
**Wait Node:** Inside each loop iteration, pauses workflow execution until the exact scheduled timestamp. n8n resumes automatically — no cron job needed.
**OpenAI Node:** Generates a time-aware caption (e.g. 'Event tomorrow!' vs 'Starting in 1 hour!') based on which slot is being processed.
**3 Parallel Posts:** LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and Facebook all post simultaneously within each loop iteration.

### 📊 Step 4 — Google Sheets Log & Telegram Notification
**Google Sheets — Append Row:** After each post within the loop, appends a row to `EventPostLog` with EventID, EventName, Platform, ScheduledTime, PostedAt, Status, and ImageURL — giving a full audit trail of every promotional post.
**Telegram — Admin Alert:** After all 3 loop iterations complete (all 9 posts dispatched — 3 slots × 3 platforms), sends a Telegram message to the admin summarising the event name, how many posts were made, and the hosted graphic URL.

nodes.name: IF — Event Has Enough Lead Time (2h+)
Edit Image — Compose Event Promo Graphic
Loop Over Items — Each Schedule Slot
Google Sheets — Append Post Log Row
Telegram — Notify Admin of Post Dispatch

main.node: Loop Over Items — Each Schedule Slot
IF — Event Has Enough Lead Time (2h+)

data.details:
The Edit Image node allows you to manipulate and edit images. Use this node when you want to:

- Blur an image
- Add a border to an image
- Create a new image
- Crop an image
- Composite an image on top of another
- Draw on an image
- Get information about the image
- Rotate an image
- Change the size of an image
- Shear an image along the X or Y axis
- Add text to the image

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
